# TagLogger Developer API

> Read tag locations and history, sync your fleet, and manage signed event webhooks.

Base URL: served at this host under /v1 (e.g. https://api.taglogger.com/v1).
Machine-readable spec: GET /v1/openapi.json (OpenAPI 3.1).

## Documentation
Human-readable guides and references are hosted at https://taglogger.com/docs:
- Quickstart:         https://taglogger.com/docs/quickstart
- Authentication:     https://taglogger.com/docs/authentication
- API keys:           https://taglogger.com/docs/api-keys
- Endpoint reference: https://taglogger.com/docs/api-reference/endpoints
- Error reference:    https://taglogger.com/docs/api-reference/errors
- Webhooks guide:     https://taglogger.com/docs/webhooks/overview
- OpenAPI spec:       https://taglogger.com/docs/openapi

## Authentication
Send a workspace API key on every request (except docs), either as:
  Authorization: Bearer <key>
or:
  X-API-Key: <key>
Live keys are prefixed tl_live_, test keys tl_test_. Keys are scoped and revocable;
the raw key is shown only once at creation.

## Scopes
- read:tags        list/read tags and their current location summary
- read:locations   precise current location, history, and fleet delta
- read:geofences   list/read geofence configuration
- manage:webhooks  create/read/update/delete/rotate/test webhook subscriptions

## Conventions
- List endpoints return { data, nextCursor }. Pass nextCursor back as ?cursor= for the next page; null means end.
- Timestamps are ISO-8601; time-range params also accept epoch milliseconds.
- Rate-limit headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset. 429 includes Retry-After.
- Errors: { "error": { "code": string, "message": string } }.
- Coordinates are suppressed while a tag's recording is paused (recordingPaused: true).

## Endpoints
GET  /v1/tags                          List tags (read:tags)
GET  /v1/tags/{tagId}                  Get a tag (read:tags)
GET  /v1/tags/{tagId}/location         Current location (read:locations)
GET  /v1/tags/{tagId}/history          Location history; params: start,end,order,limit,cursor (read:locations)
GET  /v1/fleet/delta                   Changed tags since ?since=; params: limit,cursor (read:locations)
GET  /v1/geofences                     List geofences (read:geofences)
GET  /v1/geofences/{geofenceId}        Get a geofence (read:geofences)
GET  /v1/webhooks                      List webhook subscriptions (manage:webhooks)
POST /v1/webhooks                      Create a subscription; returns signing secret once (manage:webhooks)
GET  /v1/webhooks/{webhookId}          Get a subscription (manage:webhooks)
PATCH /v1/webhooks/{webhookId}         Update url/events/label/active (manage:webhooks)
DELETE /v1/webhooks/{webhookId}        Delete a subscription (manage:webhooks)
POST /v1/webhooks/{webhookId}/rotate-secret  Rotate signing secret; returns it once (manage:webhooks)
POST /v1/webhooks/{webhookId}/test     Send a test delivery (manage:webhooks)

## Webhook events
tag.moved, tag.battery_low, tag.offline are delivered by the API event monitor.
  Delivery is off by default at launch: a new subscription begins receiving tag.*
  deliveries automatically once delivery is enabled (no changes needed on your side).
  Delivery is best-effort / at-most-once: a failed POST is NOT retried in v1, so
  reconcile against GET /v1/fleet/delta (the durable source of truth), not webhooks.
geofence.entry, geofence.exit, geofence.dwell can be subscribed to for forward
  compatibility but are NOT delivered to API-managed subscriptions in v1 (geofence
  alerts are sent only to integrations chosen in a geofence's own alert settings).

## Webhook delivery format
tag.* deliveries are HTTP POST with this JSON body:
  { "id": "<unique delivery id>", "event": "tag.moved", "occurredAt": "<ISO-8601>",
    "test": false, "tag": { "id": "<tagId>", "name": "<label|null>",
    "hardwareId": "<hwId>" }, "data": { ...event-specific... } }
Per-event data field:
  tag.moved        { from: {lat,lng}, to: {lat,lng}, distanceMeters }
  tag.battery_low  { status, critical }
  tag.offline      { lastSeenAt, staleSeconds }
Signature: header X-TagLogger-Signature: sha256=<hex HMAC-SHA256 of the raw JSON
  body using your signing secret>. Verify it before trusting a delivery.
POST /v1/webhooks/{id}/test sends a sample signed event (test:true) so you can verify
  connectivity and signature handling during onboarding.
